title: User Login with Valid Credentials
description: Verify that a user can log in successfully using valid email and password credentials and that the authentication token is stored in localStorage with protected routes accessible post-login.

steps.description: Navigate to the login page.
Input valid email and password.
Click the login button.
Verify login is successful, authentication token is stored in localStorage, and the user is redirected to a protected dashboard.

title: User Login with Invalid Credentials
description: Verify that login fails gracefully when incorrect email or password is entered and appropriate error message is shown without storing auth tokens.

steps.description: Navigate to the login page.
Input invalid email or password.
Click the login button.
Verify login failure with error message displayed and no tokens saved.

title: Authentication Token Session Persistence
description: Ensure that after logging in the authentication token and user object persist across page reloads and enable continuous access to protected routes without re-login.

steps.description: Log in with valid credentials.
Reload the page.
Verify the user remains logged in, token and user object exist in localStorage, and protected components stay accessible.

title: Profile Setup Gating for Incomplete Profiles
description: Verify that authenticated users with incomplete household or househelp profiles are redirected into profile setup flows before accessing core application features.

steps.description: Log in with a user whose profile is incomplete.
Attempt to navigate to the dashboard or other core app pages.
Verify the user is redirected to the appropriate profile setup page (household or househelp) and cannot access the core features until completion.

title: Profile Setup Completion Unlocks Access
description: Ensure that upon completing all required profile setup steps, the user gains access to their designated dashboard and core app features.

steps.description: Complete all required fields in the profile setup workflow for household or househelp.
Submit the profile setup form.
Verify the user is redirected to their dashboard and can access all role-specific features.

title: Dark and Light Theme Toggle with Persistence
description: Verify that users can toggle between dark and light themes, with the selected preference persisting across sessions via localStorage and syncing to backend for authenticated users.

steps.description: Log in or access the app.
Toggle theme between dark and light using UI controls.
Verify the theme changes immediately and persists after page reload.
For authenticated users, verify the theme preference sync with backend upon toggling.

title: Role-Based Navigation and UI Rendering
description: Ensure navigation menus and UI elements render conditionally based on user authentication state, user role (household or househelp), and display real-time counts such as shortlist, inbox, and hiring notifications.

steps.description: Log in as household user.
Verify household-specific navigation links and counts are displayed.
Log out and log in as househelp user.
Verify househelp-specific navigation is rendered appropriately with relevant counts.
Access the app as unauthenticated user.
Verify navigation only shows public marketing pages and sign-in prompts.

title: Hiring Request Creation and Workflow
description: Validate end-to-end hiring workflow for households to create requests, send contracts, and engage in negotiation with househelp, including status updates and role-specific views.

steps.description: Log in as a household user.
Navigate to hiring request creation page.
Fill out and submit a hiring request.
Verify request is created and visible in household's hiring dashboard.
Log in as househelp user.
Verify the new hiring request appears in househelp's hiring dashboard.
Househelp initiate negotiation or contract acceptance/rejection.
Verify negotiation updates are reflected in both parties' hiring dashboards in real-time.

title: Inbox Messaging and Real-Time Notifications
description: Verify that users can send and receive messages within the inbox, and that real-time notifications of new messages are received with updated unread counts via WebSocket without noticeable delay.

steps.description: Log in as household user.
Send a message to a househelp user via the inbox.
Log in as that househelp user.
Verify the househelp receives the new message notification in real-time and the unread count increments.
Open the message and mark it as read.
Verify the unread count decrements accordingly.

title: Subscription Plan Browsing and Checkout Workflow
description: Validate that users can browse subscription plans, initiate checkout, complete payment successfully, and view updated subscription status with error handling for payment failure scenarios.

steps.description: Access the subscription plans page.
Browse and select a subscription plan.
Initiate the checkout and payment process.
Verify checkout completes successfully and subscription status updates reflect the purchase.
Simulate a payment failure (e.g., declined card).
Verify error is shown and subscription status remains unchanged.

title: Public Marketing Pages Accessibility
description: Verify that all public marketing pages (home, about, services, contact, terms, privacy, cookies, pricing) load correctly, display accurate content, and are accessible without authentication.

steps.description: Navigate to each public marketing page URL as an unauthenticated user.
Verify pages load successfully with expected content and navigation elements.

title: Performance Instrumentation and Web Vitals Logging
description: Ensure web-vitals metrics are collected and logged from the client without impacting user experience or causing UI delays.

steps.description: Load the application and interact lightly with it.
Verify web-vitals metrics are recorded in logs in the expected format asynchronously.

title: SSR Express Server Route and Asset Serving
description: Confirm the Express-based SSR server correctly serves server-side rendered pages for routes, delivers static assets, and proxies API requests to backend services.

steps.description: Send requests to various app routes expecting SSR responses.
Verify server returns rendered HTML content matching requested pages.
Request static assets like CSS and JS files.
Verify assets are served with correct content-type and caching headers.
Send API requests through the server proxy endpoint.
Verify API requests are forwarded, responded to correctly, and CORS or auth headers are properly handled.

title: Routing Enforcement Using React Router Protected Routes
description: Validate that protected routes are only accessible to authenticated users and unauthenticated users are redirected appropriately.

steps.description: Attempt to access a protected route as an unauthenticated user.
Verify the user is redirected to the login or signup page.
Login as a valid user.
Access the protected route again.
Verify the protected content loads successfully.

title: Centralized API Fetch Wrapper Error Handling
description: Check that the centralized API fetch wrapper correctly handles network errors, authentication errors (e.g., 401), and returns consistent error objects to the caller.

steps.description: Make API requests with invalid or expired tokens.
Verify 401 responses are detected, session is invalidated, and user is logged out or redirected.
Simulate network errors and server errors (5xx).
Verify errors propagate to UI with user friendly error messages and retries where applicable.
